title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 September 2024.

items.title: **Two Point Museum** gets release date, exclusive Sonic pre-order items
bullets: March 4 on PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC

items.title: **Tetsuya Nomura** says he’s considering retirement, and concluding Kingdom Hearts’ story
bullets: “I’m making Kingdom Hearts 4 now with the intention of it being a story that I will complete.”

items.title: **PlayStation** announces 30th Anniversary Collection, including grey PS5 and PS5 Pro
bullets: Only 12,300 of the PS5 Pro variant will be available globally

items.title: Yager uses **Fortnite** to revive scrapped game **The Cycle: Frontier**
bullets: Using Unreal Editor for Fortnite (UEFN), the Berlin studio created an in-game location, dubbed "The Cycle: Prospect Island," which "captures the essence" of its 2022 extraction shooter.

Yager is focused on "various co-development ventures and new projects," according to a spokesperson, but said the team wanted to "keep [The Cycle's] spirit alive. This island is a way of honoring the game that many players, and us at Yager, cherished deeply."

items.title: **Midwinter** shut down following **Project T** cancellation
bullets: Midwinter Entertainment was acquired by Behaviour Interactive from Improbable in 2022 following the release of its debut title, Scavengers

"All the employees were offered to relocate to our Canadian studios, as we strongly believe in their expertise. Should they decide not to accept, they will be presented with a generous severance package. We want to thank them for all their hard work on Project T and wish them the very best for the future."

items.title: **Concord**’s director is reportedly stepping down as the game’s return remains in limbo
bullets: Ryan Ellis told staff at Firewalk Studios last week that he would no longer be serving as game director on Concord and would instead be stepping down into a support role
